quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Unit 4 Quiz 1
quiz.subtitle: Momentum, Change in Momentum, and Force-Momentum Relationship
quiz.subject: Physics

questions.question: A $1,200\text{ kg}$ car is traveling at $15\text{ m/s}$. What is its momentum?
A force of $300\text{ N}$ acts on an object for $5\text{ s}$, changing its velocity from $10\text{ m/s}$ to $25\text{ m/s}$. What is the mass of the object?
questions.explanation: $F \times t = m \times \Delta v$. $300 \times 5 = m \times (25 - 10)$. $1,500 = m \times 15$. $m = 100\text{ kg}$.

questions.question: A $0.5\text{ kg}$ ball increases its velocity from $4\text{ m/s}$ to $10\text{ m/s}$. What is its change in momentum?
A $2,000\text{ kg}$ truck slows from $20\text{ m/s}$ to $5\text{ m/s}$. What is the magnitude of its change in momentum?
questions.explanation: $\Delta p = 2,000 \times (5 - 20) = 2,000 \times (-15) = -30,000\text{ kg m/s}$. Magnitude $= 30,000\text{ kg m/s}$.

questions.question: A $0.15\text{ kg}$ baseball is pitched at $40\text{ m/s}$ and struck by a bat, reversing its direction at $50\text{ m/s}$. If the bat is in contact with the ball for $0.02\text{ s}$, what is the average force exerted?
questions.explanation: Initial $p = 0.15 \times 40 = 6.0\text{ kg m/s}$. Final $p = 0.15 \times (-50) = -7.5\text{ kg m/s}$. $\Delta p = 13.5\text{ kg m/s}$. $F = \Delta p / t = 13.5 / 0.02 = 675\text{ N}$.

questions.question: A force of $250\text{ N}$ acts on a stationary $50\text{ kg}$ crate for $4\text{ s}$. What is the impulse delivered?
questions.explanation: Impulse $= F \times t = 250 \times 4 = 1,000\text{ N s}$. This equals the change in momentum.

questions.question: A $2\text{ kg}$ object initially at rest receives an impulse of $16\text{ N s}$. What is its final velocity?
Two objects have the same momentum. Object A has twice the mass of Object B. How do their velocities compare?
options: Object A has twice the velocity of Object B
Object A has half the velocity of Object B
Object A has four times the velocity of Object B
questions.explanation: $m_A v_A = m_B v_B$. $2m_B \times v_A = m_B \times v_B$. $v_A = v_B / 2$. Object A has half the velocity.

questions.question: A $60\text{ kg}$ sprinter accelerates from $2\text{ m/s}$ to $8\text{ m/s}$ in $1.5\text{ s}$. What is the average force exerted?
questions.explanation: $\Delta p = 60 \times (8 - 2) = 360\text{ kg m/s}$. $F = \Delta p / t = 360 / 1.5 = 240\text{ N}$.

questions.question: Newton's Second Law can be expressed in terms of momentum as:
questions.explanation: Newton's Second Law states that force equals the rate of change of momentum: $F = \Delta p / t$.

questions.question: Which has more momentum: a $10\text{ kg}$ object moving at $5\text{ m/s}$ or a $2\text{ kg}$ object moving at $30\text{ m/s}$?
questions.explanation: $p_1 = 10 \times 5 = 50\text{ kg m/s}$. $p_2 = 2 \times 30 = 60\text{ kg m/s}$. The lighter, faster object has more momentum.

questions.question: A $0.4\text{ kg}$ soccer ball changes velocity from $0$ to $20\text{ m/s}$ after a kick. What is the impulse delivered to the ball?
questions.explanation: Impulse $= \Delta p = 0.4 \times (20 - 0) = 8.0\text{ kg m/s} = 8.0\text{ N s}$.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered momentum, impulse, and the force-momentum relationship!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review problems involving direction changes (reversals) in momentum.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on $p = mv$, $\Delta p = m(v_f - v_i)$, and $F = \Delta p / t$.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: momentum definition, impulse, and Newton's Second Law in momentum form.
